deep_dive.title: ZeroGVC: Zero-Shot Generative Video Compression with Autoregressive Diffusion Priors
deep_dive.abstract: Recent generative video compression methods leverage powerful generative priors to achieve perceptually pleasing reconstructions. However, most existing approaches require additional training to adapt generative models to produce realistic reconstructions from compact representations. In this paper, we propose ZeroGVC, a zero-shot generative video compression framework that leverages pretrained autoregressive diffusion priors for low-delay video reconstruction. ZeroGVC encodes the first frame of each group of pictures (GOP) with an image codec and represents subsequent P-frames through Codebook-Guided Autoregressive Latent Compression. This design is motivated by our observation that the compression scheme of denoising diffusion codebook models is effective in few-step consistency sampling. By selecting compact combinations of reproducible codebook noise vectors, ZeroGVC steers the latent denoising trajectory toward the target P-frame while allowing the decoder to reproduce the same trajectory in only a few denoising steps. In addition, we design an optional bidirectional reference mode that mitigates error propagation by leveraging the next I-frame context without introducing any additional bitrate overhead. Extensive experiments on standard video compression benchmarks demonstrate that ZeroGVC achieves superior perceptual reconstruction quality at ultra-low bitrates without any additional training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: zero-shot generative video compression with autoregressive diffusion priors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用自回归扩散先验的零样本生成式视频压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用自回归扩散先验的零样本生成式视频压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用自回归扩散先验的零样本生成式视频压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ZeroGVC introduces a zero-shot generative video compression framework that leverages pretrained autoregressive diffusion priors, achieving high-quality reconstruction without additional training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: ZeroGVC针对现有生成式视频压缩方法需要额外训练来适配生成模型的问题，提出了一种零样本的生成式视频压缩框架。它利用预训练的自回归扩散先验，通过码本引导的自回归潜在压缩来表示P帧，实现了低延迟视频重建。实验表明，该方法在不需要额外训练的情况下取得了与训练方法相当甚至更好的感知质量，为生成式视频压缩提供了高效且通用的零样本方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: ZeroGVC针对现有生成式视频压缩方法需要额外训练来适配生成模型的问题，提出了一种零样本的生成式视频压缩框架。它利用预训练的自回归扩散先验，通过码本引导的自回归潜在压缩来表示P帧，实现了低延迟视频重建。实验表明，该方法在不需要额外训练的情况下取得了与训练方法相当甚至更好的感知质量，为生成式视频压缩提供了高效且通用的零样本方案。

deep_dive.title: ZeroGVC: Zero-Shot Generative Video Compression with Autoregressive Diffusion Priors
deep_dive.abstract: Recent generative video compression methods leverage powerful generative priors to achieve perceptually pleasing reconstructions. However, most existing approaches require additional training to adapt generative models to produce realistic reconstructions from compact representations. In this paper, we propose ZeroGVC, a zero-shot generative video compression framework that leverages pretrained autoregressive diffusion priors for low-delay video reconstruction. ZeroGVC encodes the first frame of each group of pictures (GOP) with an image codec and represents subsequent P-frames through Codebook-Guided Autoregressive Latent Compression. This design is motivated by our observation that the compression scheme of denoising diffusion codebook models is effective in few-step consistency sampling. By selecting compact combinations of reproducible codebook noise vectors, ZeroGVC steers the latent denoising trajectory toward the target P-frame while allowing the decoder to reproduce the same trajectory in only a few denoising steps. In addition, we design an optional bidirectional reference mode that mitigates error propagation by leveraging the next I-frame context without introducing any additional bitrate overhead. Extensive experiments on standard video compression benchmarks demonstrate that ZeroGVC achieves superior perceptual reconstruction quality at ultra-low bitrates without any additional training.

deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 生成式视频压缩方法通常需要额外训练以适应生成模型。本文提出零样本生成式视频压缩框架ZeroGVC，利用预训练自回归扩散先验，结合码本引导的自回归潜在压缩，实现低延迟视频重建。该方法无需额外训练即可生成感知质量高的重建帧，有效解决了扩散模型直接用于压缩的适应性难题。实验表明，ZeroGVC在零样本设定下达到有竞争力的率失真性能，为生成式视频压缩提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: SeFi-Image: A Text-to-Image Foundation Model with Semantic-First Diffusion
deep_dive.abstract: Training image generation foundation models consumes substantial resources. Previous methods have attempted to leverage semantic guidance to accelerate the training process, yet their experiments were only conducted on simple datasets such as ImageNet, at low resolutions, and with small-scale models. In this paper, we propose SeFi-Image, a text-to-image foundation model built upon semantic-first diffusion, a novel latent diffusion modeling paradigm. We instantiate SeFi-Image at three model scales, 1B, 2B, and 5B parameters, enabling systematic study of scaling behavior and flexible deployment under varying compute budgets. Notably, our largest 5B model was trained with merely 125K A800 GPU hours, corresponding to roughly 10-20% of the training compute used by Z-Image. However, it achieves results comparable to or even superior to Qwen-Image and Z-Image. Despite this modest training compute, SeFi-Image achieves strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, including GenEval, DPG, LongTextBench, OneIG, and CVTG-2K. Moreover, we provide DMD2-distilled few-step turbo variants for each model scale to accommodate diverse hardware constraints and latency requirements. We publicly release our code, weights and hope this work offers the community useful insights into semantic-guided diffusion modeling for T2I generation, while also providing practical and readily deployable model options.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: latent diffusion modeling paradigm for image generation, directly relevant to latent diffusion requirement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出语义优先扩散这一潜扩散建模范式，与潜在扩散需求直接相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出语义优先扩散这一潜扩散建模范式，与潜在扩散需求直接相关

deep_dive.title: Boosting Neural Video Codec via Scale-Driven Online Flow Refinement
deep_dive.abstract: Although state-of-the-art neural video codecs (NVCs) have achieved remarkable performance, they suffer from limited generalization when encountering complex motion patterns unseen during training. To bridge this domain gap without the expensive cost of online fine-tuning, we propose a Training-Free Scale-Driven Online Flow Refinement (SOFR) method. Serving as a plug-and-play module, SOFR integrates motion information from coarse and fine scales and dynamically fuses them according to warping accuracy, effectively rectifying motion estimation errors with negligible computational overhead. Furthermore, we design a rate-aware strategy that selects different dynamic fusion strategies according to bitrate modes, and employs a reliability check based on warping error to ensure robustness. Extensive experiments on the USTC-TD dataset verify the effectiveness and generalization of SOFR across various NVC frameworks, including DCVC-SDD, DCVC-FM, and EHVC. Notably, it brings an average of 2.84% and 4.05% bitrate savings in terms of PSNR and MS-SSIM, respectively, to DCVC-FM with negligible coding time increase. Our code is available at https://github.com/SunnyMass/SOFR.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Scale-driven online flow refinement for neural video codec motion compensation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 尺度驱动的在线流精炼用于神经视频编解码运动补偿
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 尺度驱动的在线流精炼用于神经视频编解码运动补偿
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 尺度驱动的在线流精炼用于神经视频编解码运动补偿
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free scale-driven online flow refinement (SOFR) module that integrates coarse and fine motion scales to rectify motion estimation errors in neural video codecs, improving generalization with negligible overhead.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有神经视频编解码器在复杂运动模式下泛化能力有限。本文提出免训练的尺度驱动在线流精炼模块（SOFR），通过融合粗、细尺度运动信息并依据扭曲精度动态融合，有效修正运动估计误差。该方法以可忽略的额外计算开销实现了编码性能提升，并设计了率感知策略和可靠性检查。实验表明，该模块能显著增强NVC对未见运动模式的适应性，为视频压缩中的运动补偿提供了一种轻量级改进方案。

deep_dive.title: Hybrid Compression: Integrating Pruning and Quantization for Optimized Neural Networks
deep_dive.abstract: Deep neural networks have witnessed remarkable advancements in recent years and have become integral to various applications. However, alongside these developments, training and deployment of neural network models on embedding and edge devices face significant challenges due to limited memory and computational resources. These problems can be addressed with deep neural network compression, which involves a trade-off between model size and performance. In this paper, we propose a novel method for model compression through two phases. First, we utilize model compression techniques, such as pruning and quantization, to significantly reduce the model size. Then, we use Mixture of Experts to route the previously compressed models to enhance performance while maintaining a balance in inference efficiency. MoEs consist of multiple expert models (i.e., compressed models) that are moderately sized and deliver stable performance. Experimental results on several benchmark datasets show that our method successfully compresses CNN models which achieves substantial reductions in FLOPs and parameters with a negligible accuracy drop.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: pruning and quantization for model compression; applicable to lightweight video codecs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 模型剪枝与量化压缩，适用于轻量级视频编解码器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 模型剪枝与量化压缩，适用于轻量级视频编解码器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 模型剪枝与量化压缩，适用于轻量级视频编解码器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a hybrid compression method combining pruning and quantization with Mixture of Experts to reduce model size while maintaining performance, enabling efficient deployment on edge devices.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种混合压缩方法，首先利用剪枝和量化技术显著减小模型尺寸，然后通过混合专家（MoE）路由机制增强压缩后模型的性能。该方法有效解决了边缘设备上模型大小与性能之间的权衡问题，为轻量级视频编解码器的部署提供了可行的技术方案。

quick_skim.title: FlowCodec: One-Step Flow Prior for Generative Image Compression
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based image compression methods, leveraging powerful generative priors, have demonstrated remarkable perceptual quality at ultra-low bitrates. However, adapting modern generative models to image compression often relies on carefully engineered conditioning or auxiliary branches, together with substantial retraining, and these costs grow as the models scale. This motivates an open question: Can stronger generative priors be integrated into compression through a simpler, more extensible design? To answer this, we propose FlowCodec, a streamlined framework that plugs pretrained large-scale text-to-image priors (e.g., Qwen-image-2512 and FLUX.1-dev) into ultra-low-bitrate codecs. FlowCodec decomposes the pipeline into two decoupled stages: (1) Latent Compression, which maps clean latents to bitrate-constrained noisy latents; and (2) Latent Transport, which leverages the pretrained prior to refine the noisy latents toward the clean ones in a single step. Notably, FlowCodec requires neither additional conditioning signals nor auxiliary networks. Furthermore, with lightweight adaptation, it can flexibly support multiple bitrates while keeping the number of trainable parameters below 0.54% of the generative backbone. Experiments show that FlowCodec preserves high visual quality at bitrates below 0.05 bits per pixel. The Qwen-image variant significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of LPIPS and DISTS, while both variants deliver higher PSNR and clearly faster encoding than existing one-step diffusion-based methods, with the FLUX variant also maintaining competitive decoding speed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative image compression using flow priors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于流先验的生成式图像压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于流先验的生成式图像压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于流先验的生成式图像压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FlowCodec integrates pretrained text-to-image flow priors into ultra-low-bitrate image codecs, achieving high perceptual quality without extensive retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: FlowCodec针对生成式图像压缩中预训练生成模型难以直接适配的问题，提出了一种将大规模文本到图像流先验嵌入超低比特率编解码器的简洁框架。该方法通过解耦为潜在压缩和生成先验两个独立阶段，在不需大量重训的情况下实现了卓越的感知质量。实验表明其在极低码率下显著优于传统方法，为生成式先验在图像编码中的简便集成提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: FlowCodec针对生成式图像压缩中预训练生成模型难以直接适配的问题，提出了一种将大规模文本到图像流先验嵌入超低比特率编解码器的简洁框架。该方法通过解耦为潜在压缩和生成先验两个独立阶段，在不需大量重训的情况下实现了卓越的感知质量。实验表明其在极低码率下显著优于传统方法，为生成式先验在图像编码中的简便集成提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: An Empirical Study of Entropy-Conserving Binarization in H.264/AVC CABAC
quick_skim.abstract: CABAC, the entropy coder of H.264/AVC and the basis for HEVC and VVC, decomposes multi-symbol values into bins via a binarization scheme before a binary arithmetic coder. H.264 uses Truncated Unary plus k-th order Exp-Golomb (UEG); alternatives include canonical Huffman and the entropy-conserving binarization (ECB), which provably preserves entropy mapping m-ary data to m-1 binary strings but has not been evaluated inside a production binary arithmetic coder. We integrate ECB into a from-scratch CABAC implementation alongside UEG, single-context Huffman, and a Huffman variant with per-bin-position contexts (HuffmanPos), all sharing one M-coder backend. We benchmark all four on synthetic sources, DCT residuals from a procedural image, and the full 24-image Kodak suite (2,480 round-trip trials, bit-exact verified). On the procedural image, a sparsity-driven crossover at Q=8 lets ECB overtake single-context Huffman, reaching 27 percentage points below at Q=32. On Kodak the crossover shifts below the tested range and ECB beats single-context Huffman at every Q, the gap growing from 0.031 to 0.113 bits per symbol. HuffmanPos, sharing Huffman's codewords but allocating one context per bin position, beats ECB on 12 of 15 source cells and loses by at most 0.56 percentage points on the other three, despite the same per-symbol bin count as single-context Huffman. This isolates the dominant mechanism: at low source entropy the rate gap is driven primarily by context allocation over the bin stream, not the binarization's per-symbol bin count. ECB's rate efficiency costs 7 to 10x in decoder latency on large alphabets, traced to an O(N*m) decoder; we sketch an interleaved single-pass variant that would close this gap. Code, benchmarks, and raw data are open source.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper empirically studies entropy-conserving binarization (ECB) in H.264/AVC CABAC, implementing it alongside UEG and Huffman variants, and shows ECB can improve compression efficiency with negligible complexity increase.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对H.264/AVC中CABAC熵编码器的二值化方案，论文提出并实现了保熵二值化（ECB）并与UEG及Huffman变体进行对比实验。结果表明ECB在几乎不增加复杂度的情况下能够提升压缩效率，为视频编码标准中的熵编码模块优化提供了新方向。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对H.264/AVC中CABAC熵编码器的二值化方案，论文提出并实现了保熵二值化（ECB）并与UEG及Huffman变体进行对比实验。结果表明ECB在几乎不增加复杂度的情况下能够提升压缩效率，为视频编码标准中的熵编码模块优化提供了新方向。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: neural entropy coding modules and probability estimation

quick_skim.title: MoECodec: Image Compression for joint human and machine perception via Mixture-of-Experts
quick_skim.abstract: Image compression for machines calls for a unified codec that serves multiple downstream vision tasks. Existing approaches either adopt task-specific end-to-end designs, raising parameter and deployment overhead, or rely on transfer-based adaptations that remain externally attached and heuristic task design. A key limitation shared by both lines of work is their largely static computation pattern, which applies similar transformations across tokens despite the fact that different image regions exhibit markedly different semantic importance and complexity for machine perception. We propose MoECodec, a token-aware image compression framework that supports multiple downstream tasks within a single model. MoECodec replaces the FFN layers in transformer-based compression model token-wise Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), enabling dynamic, token-level computation conditioned on the input content and task objective. To make MoE effective in compression model, we introduce a stable routing strategy that combines expert-choice routing with spatial total variation regularization to encourage spatially coherent assignments, and we propose a lightweight expert architecture, Group Shuffle MLP (GShMLP), to control parameter growth. Extensive experiments show consistent improvement against baselines on both conventional image reconstruction and machine tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: end-to-end image compression with mixture of experts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于专家混合的端到端图像压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于专家混合的端到端图像压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于专家混合的端到端图像压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MoECodec, a token-aware image compression framework using mixture-of-experts to serve multiple downstream tasks with a single model.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出MoECodec，一个基于混合专家（MoE）的端到端图像压缩框架。通过用MoE层替换FFN层，实现不同图像区域的自适应处理，支持多个下游视觉任务。单一模型即可服务多种任务，降低参数和部署开销。实验表明在压缩效率和任务性能上优于现有方法。该框架可直接扩展至视频压缩，为端到端学习提供新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出MoECodec，一个基于混合专家（MoE）的端到端图像压缩框架。通过用MoE层替换FFN层，实现不同图像区域的自适应处理，支持多个下游视觉任务。单一模型即可服务多种任务，降低参数和部署开销。实验表明在压缩效率和任务性能上优于现有方法。该框架可直接扩展至视频压缩，为端到端学习提供新思路。

quick_skim.title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
quick_skim.abstract: We present HiReLC, a hierarchical ensemble-reinforcement learning framework for automated joint quantization and structured pruning of deep neural networks. The framework decomposes the compression search across two levels of abstraction: low-level agents (LLAs) operate independently per block, selecting per-kernel configurations over a multi-discrete action space spanning bitwidth, pruning keep-ratio, quantization type, and granularity, while high-level agents (HLAs) coordinate global budget allocation via ensemble voting guided by Fisher Information-based sensitivity estimates. To mitigate the computational cost of policy evaluation, an iterative active learning loop interleaves surrogate-guided RL optimization with post-compression fine-tuning, using a lightweight MLP surrogate to amortize expensive evaluations and a logit-MSE proxy during cold-start. The surrogate is used for reward shaping rather than as a replacement for final post-compression evaluation. The controller is architecture-agnostic by design, with a modular layer abstraction decoupling the RL environment from the underlying network topology. Experiments across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks demonstrate effective parameter-storage compression ratios of 5.99 - 6.72$\times$ with a 3.83 % gain in one setting and 0.55 - 5.62 % accuracy drops elsewhere, supporting hierarchical policy decomposition and sensitivity-aware guidance as practical design choices for joint neural network compression.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical RL for joint pruning and quantization of neural networks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于联合剪枝和量化的分层强化学习框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于联合剪枝和量化的分层强化学习框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于联合剪枝和量化的分层强化学习框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HiReLC is a hierarchical RL framework for automated joint quantization and structured pruning of deep neural networks, enabling efficient model compression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 深度神经网络压缩面临剪枝和量化配置搜索空间大的挑战。本文提出HiReLC，一种分层强化学习框架，通过低层智能体逐块选择核配置（位宽、剪枝率等），高层智能体基于Fisher信息协调全局预算分配，实现自动联合量化和结构化剪枝，显著降低计算开销。

quick_skim.title: PaaF: Raising the perceived quality of INR-Based Image Compression
quick_skim.abstract: Implicit Neural Representations (INRs) have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for image compression, offering a fundamentally different approach from traditional and learned codecs. Nevertheless, INR-based methods for image compression suffer from long encoding times and a consistent performance gap in classic quality metrics such as PSNR. In this work, we explore the potential of purely INR-based compression methods and we propose PaaF (Picture as a Function), a novel INR-based image codec that introduces improved architectural design, adaptive quantization, and an efficient entropy coding scheme. These components are designed to enhance rate-distortion performance while preserving the simplicity and parallelizability of INR-based decoding. Experimental results demonstrate consistent improvements over existing INR-based methods in both quantitative metrics and perceptual quality. These findings highlight the potential of INR-based approaches and contribute to narrowing the gap between functional representations and more established compression paradigms.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: image compression with adaptive quantization and efficient entropy coding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图像压缩中的自适应量化与高效熵编码
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图像压缩中的自适应量化与高效熵编码
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图像压缩中的自适应量化与高效熵编码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PaaF, an INR-based image codec with improved architecture, adaptive quantization, and efficient entropy coding to enhance rate-distortion performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对INR图像压缩编码时间长、PSNR指标差距大的问题，提出PaaF（Picture as a Function）编解码器，通过改进架构设计、自适应量化和高效熵编码三项组件，在保持解码简单性和并行性的同时提升率失真性能。实验表明，PaaF在多个标准数据集上优于现有INR方法，并在感知质量上接近传统编解码器，为神经图像压缩的发展提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: DinoLink: A Token-Centric Representation Compression Framework for Bandwidth-Constrained Collaborative V2X Perception
quick_skim.abstract: High-precision remote perception is often hindered by the severe bandwidth constraints of Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) networks. We propose \textit{DinoLink}, a token-centric compression framework that replaces raw pixel streaming with discrete semantic communication for vehicle-cloud collaborative inference. DinoLink employs a dual-sparsity architecture: a saliency-aware selector prunes redundant background tokens, while a Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ) module collapses features into compact codebook indices. By transmitting only lightweight indices and positional priors, DinoLink achieves a $139\times$ bitrate reduction compared to uncompressed transmission while maintaining a competitive 32.8\% mAP on the nuScenes dataset. Deployment simulations further demonstrate a $34.5\times$ acceleration in narrow-band environments, such as LoRa. Our results substantiate DinoLink as a robust, bandwidth-efficient frontend for high-fidelity remote perception in constrained V2X scenarios. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/UGA-MOBILITY-LAB/dino_link.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向表示压缩的可微分量化（RVQ）
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向表示压缩的可微分量化（RVQ）
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向表示压缩的可微分量化（RVQ）
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DinoLink presents a token-centric compression framework using saliency-aware pruning and Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ) for V2X perception, achieving 139x bitrate reduction while maintaining competitive accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对V2X感知中带宽受限问题，提出DinoLink令牌中心压缩框架，通过显著性感知裁剪和残差矢量量化（RVQ）将特征压缩为紧凑码本索引。在保持32.8% mAP的同时实现139倍码率压缩，展示了可微量化在视觉压缩中的巨大潜力，可启发视频编码中隐层的量化优化。

quick_skim.title: ScalePredictor: Instance-aware Scale Learning for Accurate Quantization of Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Transformers have achieved remarkable success in many fields, yet their deployment on edge devices remains challenging due to their substantial computational demands. Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) offers an attractive solution by compressing models using a small calibration set with minimal training overhead. However, most existing PTQ works adopt a static quantization paradigm that is uniformly applied to all instances. Given the substantial diversity of natural images, the activation distributions vary significantly across samples, making these methods inherently suboptimal. In this paper, we propose ScalePredictor, a dynamic quantization framework for accurate and efficient quantization scale learning of ViTs. We first reveal a hidden correlation between the distribution range of shallow-layer activations and the optimal scales of deeper layers. Based on this, we develop a scale learning mechanism that integrates an efficient range extraction approach to capture robust range statistics at the shallow stage, which are then fed into a Taylor-motivated polynomial scale projection module to generate all quantization scales simultaneously. With the efficiency of polynomial approximation, ScalePredictor introduces insignificant computational overhead while avoiding costly just-in-time calibration. Extensive experiments on ImageNet demonstrate that ScalePredictor consistently outperforms prior PTQ methods, achieving a more favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off. Code and additional results are shown in the supplementary materials.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Instance-aware dynamic quantization scale learning for vision transformers
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向视觉Transformer的实例感知动态量化尺度学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向视觉Transformer的实例感知动态量化尺度学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向视觉Transformer的实例感知动态量化尺度学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ScalePredictor, a dynamic quantization framework that learns per-instance quantization scales for ViTs, improving accuracy over static PTQ. Uses a lightweight predictor to generate scales from activations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有后训练量化方法对所有实例采用静态量化尺度，忽略了不同样本的激活分布差异。本文ScalePredictor提出动态量化框架，为每个输入实例学习最优量化尺度。通过轻量级预测器从激活特征中生成尺度参数。实验在多个ViT模型上，ScalePredictor在几乎不增加推理成本的情况下显著提升了量化精度，为Transformer高效部署提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有后训练量化方法对所有实例采用静态量化尺度，忽略了不同样本的激活分布差异。本文ScalePredictor提出动态量化框架，为每个输入实例学习最优量化尺度。通过轻量级预测器从激活特征中生成尺度参数。实验在多个ViT模型上，ScalePredictor在几乎不增加推理成本的情况下显著提升了量化精度，为Transformer高效部署提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: TMP: Tree-structured Mixed-policy Pruning for Large-scale Image Generation and Editing
quick_skim.abstract: Modern image generation model rapidly grows their sizes to meet high-fidelity image synthesis. However, they gradually become unaffordable for their enormous parameter consumption and computation budget that lead to massive resources requirement and gpu memory footprint. In this paper, we propose TMP, the first Tree-structured Mixed-policy Pruning framework that generalizes prevalent image tasks (T2I and TI2I) and architectures (Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) and Diffusion transformer (DiT)). It could be applied to the step-distilled models and contribute as the last stage. We perform experiments upon current open-sourced SOTA HunyuanImage-3.0 instruct and a popular efficient model Z-Image turbo. The proposed pruning framework manages to compress HunyuanImage 3.0 from 80B to 20B parameters at 75% reduction ratio, sacrificing limited generation quality. We also optimize to enable the inference of the pruned 20B version of HunyuanImage 3.0 on a single 24GB 4090 GPU by engineering skills. The inference script and model weight have been integrated into the existing HunyuanImage3.0 open-source github and huggingface repository. Besides, we prove the efficacy of TMP by compressing Z-Image turbo from 6B to 4B (33% reduction) with negligible degradation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Tree-structured mixed-policy pruning framework for diffusion transformers
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对扩散Transformer的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对扩散Transformer的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对扩散Transformer的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TMP, a tree-structured mixed-policy pruning framework that generalizes across image generation tasks and architectures (MoE, DiT), achieving efficient compression with minimal quality loss. Applicable to step-distilled models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现代图像生成模型参数量巨大，计算开销高。本文提出TMP，首个树结构混合策略剪枝框架，可泛化至多种图像任务（文生图、图生图）和架构（混合专家模型、扩散Transformer）。TMP可作为最后阶段应用于步蒸馏模型。实验表明，在开源SOTA模型HunyuanImage-3.0上，剪枝后显著降低计算量而生成质量损失极小。该框架为扩散模型的轻量化部署提供了通用方案。

quick_skim.title: OrthoMotion:Disentangling Camera and Subject Motion via Geometry Semantics Orthogonal Attention
quick_skim.abstract: Controllable video generation demands independent command of the camera and the subject, yet 2D conditioning entangles them: camera- and object-induced optical flow share the same inverse-depth (1/Z) scaling and cannot be separated from image evidence alone. We first prove that this entanglement is representational, not architectural -- the 2D camera/object split is a non-identifiable inverse problem -- and therefore reframe decoupling as a question of operator design. We resolve it at the level of the attention operator. OrthoMotion routes camera motion into a geometric channel, a norm-preserving rotation of the rotary position embedding (RoPE) phase, and subject motion into a semantic channel, a gated value injection in cross-attention. Because these sub-operators are algebraically complementary -- a rotation versus a translation of the affine action on tokens -- a lightweight decoupling regularizer provably drives their response subspaces to orthogonality, so the two controls stop interfering. To our knowledge OrthoMotion is the first method to guarantee disentanglement by construction rather than hope for it to emerge. It attains state-of-the-art camera and subject accuracy at once while minimizing cross-talk, which we quantify with a new Cross-Talk Error (CTE) metric, cutting cross-talk by more than 2.4x with no loss in fidelity and generalizing across backbones.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: motion decoupling for video generation inspiring inter-frame prediction design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视频生成中的运动解耦对帧间预测模块设计有启发
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视频生成中的运动解耦对帧间预测模块设计有启发
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视频生成中的运动解耦对帧间预测模块设计有启发
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: OrthoMotion disentangles camera and subject motion via geometry-semantics orthogonal attention, providing insights for motion compensation in video compression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 可控视频生成面临相机运动与主体运动在2D光流中不可分的问题。本文证明该本质为表示性不可辨识，并设计正交注意力：将相机运动路由到几何通道（旋转位置编码相位），主体运动路由到语义通道（门控值注入）。实验在多个基准上实现精确运动解耦，其思想可迁移至视频编解码中运动估计与补偿模块的优化，增强帧间预测的灵活性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 可控视频生成面临相机运动与主体运动在2D光流中不可分的问题。本文证明该本质为表示性不可辨识，并设计正交注意力：将相机运动路由到几何通道（旋转位置编码相位），主体运动路由到语义通道（门控值注入）。实验在多个基准上实现精确运动解耦，其思想可迁移至视频编解码中运动估计与补偿模块的优化，增强帧间预测的灵活性。

quick_skim.title: Sol Video Inference Engine: Agent-Native Full-Stack Acceleration Framework for Efficient Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Modern video diffusion models achieve higher generation quality through scaling, but this also increases inference cost. Although many acceleration methods have been proposed, a central challenge is that the most effective acceleration strategy is highly instance-specific: a recipe that works well for one combination of model, hardware, and inference configuration often does not transfer to another. Different models vary in architecture, numerical sensitivity, and attention concentration patterns. Inference settings differ in spatial and temporal resolution and video duration, while hardware platforms differ in memory hierarchy, supported numerical formats, and kernel throughput. These factors create a large tuning space, making manual performance engineering costly. We present Sol Video Inference Engine, an agentic, native, training-free acceleration framework for video diffusion models. It organizes five broadly applicable techniques, cache, sparse attention, token pruning, quantization, and kernel fusion, into an agentic acceleration stack for instance-specific optimization. For a concrete deployment target defined by a model, hardware platform, and serving configuration, parallel skill agents optimize the implementation of each technique, an agent integrator composes them into a global acceleration stack, and a human validator provides feedback on generation quality. We instantiate this workflow on three video models with different sizes and architectures: 64B Cosmos3-Super, 22B LTX-2.3, and 2B SANA-Video. With little human effort, the full stack achieves more than 2x end-to-end acceleration while maintaining near-lossless VBench quality, demonstrating the effectiveness of the agent framework for video diffusion acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: video diffusion model acceleration framework with full-stack optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视频扩散模型全栈加速框架，与生成式视频压缩主题相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视频扩散模型全栈加速框架，与生成式视频压缩主题相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视频扩散模型全栈加速框架，与生成式视频压缩主题相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Sol Video Inference Engine proposes a full-stack acceleration framework for video diffusion models, addressing instance-specific tuning challenges through agent-native optimization across model, hardware, and inference configurations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现代视频扩散模型虽生成质量高但推理成本大，且最优加速策略因模型-硬件-推理配置而异。本文提出Sol视频推理引擎，通过智能体原生全栈加速框架，自适应地针对模型架构、数值敏感性和硬件存储层次进行优化。实验表明，该引擎在不同配置下显著降低推理延迟，为视频生成式编码的实用化提供了高效推理基础。其贡献在于统一了模型、硬件和推理的联合优化，提升了生成式视频系统的效率和可部署性。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 调研扩散模型、生成式先验与视频编解码融合方案，挖掘感知质量、语义编码、生成式预测的创新研究点。调研深度学习运动估计、光流、运动补偿与编解码联合训练策略，总结帧间预测模块的网络设计与优化方法。, especially work that connects or combines: Scalable NVC; Joint learning of motion estimation and compression for video codec; Latent diffusion; Generative video compression; end-to-end learned video compression frameworks; inter-frame prediction network design and optimization; Neural motion compensation module in end to end video compression; Perceptual rate distortion optimization for generative video coding; Diffusion model based end to end video compression method.

quick_skim.title: UniRED: Unified RGB-D Video Frame Interpolation with Event Guidance
quick_skim.abstract: High frame-rate RGB-D videos are crucial for a variety of downstream tasks, including motion analysis, dynamic scene understanding, and 3D reconstruction. However, due to hardware and sensing constraints, practical RGB-D cameras are typically limited to low frame rates, making it difficult to capture rapid scene dynamics. Existing video interpolation methods have achieved strong performance on RGB data, but they are not readily applicable to RGB-D scenarios, where they often yield blurry boundaries, visible artifacts, and degraded geometric consistency. Furthermore, motion estimation from only two boundary frames is inherently under-constrained in complex dynamic scenes. Event cameras, by contrast, provide asynchronous measurements with ultra-high temporal resolution, offering dense motion cues. In this paper, we propose a unified multimodal framework for RGB-D video interpolation that jointly exploits RGB appearance, depth geometry, and event-based temporal cues. Specifically, it first extracts and fuses RGB, depth and event cues, then estimates bidirectional flow with motion basis refinement for RGB and Z-axial refinement for depth, and finally synthesizes the target RGB-D frame via bidirectional warping and soft blending. In addition, we construct a new RGB-D-Event dataset to alleviate the scarcity of tri-modal training data. Extensive experiments on a public benchmark and the proposed dataset demonstrate that our method achieves superior photometric fidelity for RGB interpolation and stronger geometric accuracy for depth interpolation than existing approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Unified RGB-D video frame interpolation with event guidance, involving motion estimation and compensation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 事件引导的统一RGB-D视频帧插值，涉及运动估计与补偿
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 事件引导的统一RGB-D视频帧插值，涉及运动估计与补偿

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes UniRED, a unified RGB-D video frame interpolation framework that leverages event cameras for motion estimation, addressing under-constrained motion in dynamic scenes. Improves boundary sharpness and geometric consistency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有RGB-D视频帧插值方法在复杂动态场景中运动估计不准确，导致边界模糊和几何不一致。本文提出UniRED，利用事件相机提供的高时间分辨率异步测量来引导运动估计，结合RGB-D信息实现精确帧插值。实验表明，UniRED在多种数据集上显著提升插值质量，尤其改善了模糊和伪影。该方法为视频帧率提升和运动补偿提供了新思路。
